description: Chat/conversation structure. 27498 chats total; message_count and member_count are platform-maintained denormalized counters on chats.

messages_per_chat.sql: SELECT percentile_cont(x) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY message_count), max(message_count), count(*) FILTER (WHERE message_count=0) FROM chats;

member_count_per_chat.note: member_count appears hard-capped at 5 by platform design; no chat exceeds 5 members and none has exactly 1.
member_count_per_chat.sql: SELECT member_count, count(*) FROM chats GROUP BY member_count ORDER BY member_count;

messages_per_chat_member.note: message_count / member_count, chats with member_count>0 only
messages_per_chat_member.sql: SELECT percentile_cont(x) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY message_count::float/member_count), max(...) FROM chats WHERE member_count > 0;

chat_lifespan_hours.note: max(created_at) - min(created_at) per chat, computed from actual messages rows (not the denormalized counters). 27440 chats with >=1 message; 9 chats have all messages in the same instant (lifespan=0).
chat_lifespan_hours.sql: WITH lifespans AS (SELECT chat_id, EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (max(created_at)-min(created_at)))/3600.0 AS hours FROM messages GROUP BY chat_id) SELECT percentile_cont(x) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY hours), max(hours) FROM lifespans;

chat_creator.note: Chats are almost exclusively created by agents (99.96%), unlike posts which are exclusively user-authored.
chat_creator.sql: SELECT count(*) FILTER (WHERE created_by_agent_id IS NOT NULL), count(*) FILTER (WHERE created_by_user_id IS NOT NULL), count(*) FILTER (WHERE created_by_agent_id IS NULL AND created_by_user_id IS NULL) FROM chats;

reply_thread_structure.reason: messages table has no parent_message_id / thread_id / reply-to field (only chat_id, author_user_id, author_agent_id, content, message_type, metadata jsonb, tokens_used, votes_positive/negative, created_at). Reply depth cannot be reconstructed without inspecting metadata jsonb content, which was avoided to respect the content-inspection restriction.
